results.title: Earnings call transcript: Vale Q4 2025 results miss EPS forecasts
highlights:
# Earnings call transcript: Vale Q4 2025 results miss EPS forecasts

By

Transcripts Published 02/13/2026, 12:24 PM

Earnings call transcript: Vale Q4 2025 results miss EPS forecasts View all comments (0)0

Earnings call transcript: Vale Q4 2025 results miss EPS forecasts

VALE2.49% Vale SA (VALE) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, revealing a significant miss on earnings per share (EPS) expectations, while revenue exceeded forecasts. The company’s EPS was -$0.1255, falling short of the anticipated $0.587, resulting in a surprising negative deviation of 121.38%. In contrast, revenue reached $11.06 billion, surpassing the forecasted $10.83 billion by 2.12%. The market reacted negatively, with Vale’s stock price dropping 2.2% to $16.76 in pre-market  ...    - Vale aims for significant growth in copper production through new projects. - The company announced a $2.8 billion dividend and interest on capital distribution. - Vale’s iron ore and base metals segments showed strong EBITDA growth. - The stock dropped 2.2% in pre-market trading following the earnings release. - Vale posted a substantial EPS miss but exceeded revenue expectations.  ...    Vale demonstrated strong operational performance in Q4 2025, with a 17% year-over-year increase in pro forma EBITDA, reaching $4.8 billion. Management reaffirmed 2026 guidance and emphasized growth in copper and iron ore, underpinned by disciplined CapEx and high-return projects. Thanks, Gustavo, and good morning, everyone. As Gustavo highlighted in his opening remarks, 2025 was an outstanding year for Vale. With strong performance and consistent execution across all three businesses, we delivered robust results and entered 2026 with great confidence and clear momentum. In the fourth quarter of 2025, our pro forma EBITDA reached $4.8 billion, representing an increase of 17% year-on-year and 10% quarter-on-quarter. As shown on the slide, this strong performance was primarily driven by an excellent quarter at Vale Base Metals, supported by favorable pricing conditions for copper and by-products, while continuing to capture meaningful operational gains across our polymetallic operations in Canada. As a result, Vale Base Metals EBITDA more than doubled both year-on-year and sequentially, reaching $1.4 billion in the quarter, clearly demonstrating improved operating  performance as well as the earnings power of this business. In iron ore, we also delivered strong results, with EBITDA remaining at a solid $4 billion, with higher sales volumes and improved realized prices compensating for the BRL appreciation in the quarter. results.author: GuruFocus News      February 13, 2026   4 min read
highlights:
Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Production Growth and Financial Performance HG=F +1.16%

VALE +1.96%

This article first appeared on GuruFocus. Iron Ore Production: 336 million tons in 2025, a 3% increase year on year. Copper Production: 382,000 tons in 2025, a 10% increase year on year. Nickel Production: 177,000 tons in 2025, an 11% increase year on year. Pro Forma EBITDA: $4.8 billion in Q4 2025, a 17% increase year on year. Valley-Based Metals EBITDA: $1.4 billion in Q4 2025, more than doubled year on year. All-in Costs for Iron Ore: $54 per ton in 2025, a $2 per ton reduction year on year. All-in Costs for Copper: Decreased by $2,000 per ton, reaching -$0.9,000 per ton. All-in Costs for Nickel: Declined 35% year on year, reaching $9,000 per ton. Recurring Free Cash Flow: Approximately $1.7 billion in Q4 2025.

CapEx: $5.5 billion in 2025, in line with guidance. Dividends and Interest on Capital: $2.8 billion announced, with $1 billion paid in January. Net Debt: Reduced to $15.6 billion by the end of 2025.
